summary: Implementation is clean and correct. Prisma fully removed, Payload REST API integrated properly. require.main guard prevents test side effects. Export surface adequate for testing.

findings.finding: PAYLOAD_API_KEY is re-resolved on every savePost() call via process.env lookup + IIFE. This is intentional (test isolation) but could be extracted to a helper getPayloadApiKey() for clarity.
findings.reason: Inline resolution is explicit and self-documenting via comment. Extracting to helper adds no functional value in this file scope.

findings.finding: resolveTagIds uses .filter(Boolean) which has return type string[] but TypeScript infers (string | undefined)[]. Works at runtime but could be typed more precisely with .filter((id): id is string => Boolean(id)).
findings.reason: TypeScript compiles cleanly (npx tsc --noEmit passes). The .filter(Boolean) pattern is idiomatic and accepted by the compiler here.

findings.finding: main() does not validate PAYLOAD_API_KEY before starting the loop — if key is missing, first savePost() call will throw and the error will be caught by the catch handler. This is acceptable but an early check in main() would give a cleaner startup message.
findings.reason: The error message 'PAYLOAD_API_KEY not set in environment' is clear enough. Adding early validation would change the accepted spec pattern.
